meaning: adj.抽着鼻子的,假装虔诚的
examples: I could hear the child snuffling in her sleep.
Tom was snuffling, now, himself—and more in pity of himself than anybody else.
A kind of snuffling, an animal sound.
Our Freddy had begun snuffling close behind groups of heli-tourists, perhaps smelling snack food.
For devotees, it will signal a fond and snuffling farewell, as they bid adieu to a vast parade of clones, Ewoks, Darths, tragic hair styles, noble knights, and strangely fastidious androids.
